answer:
According to the Formulary Drug List, formulary drugs are those included in the list of covered drugs under your plan. They are categorized into four tiers: Preferred Generic (1), Preferred Brand (2), Preferred Specialty (3), and Non-Preferred (4). The list is developed by an independent committee of physicians, pharmacists, and other healthcare providers to ensure safety, effectiveness, and cost-effectiveness.

Formulary drugs include:

* Preferred Generic (Tier 1): lowest tier, includes generic drugs
* Preferred Brand (Tier 2): includes brand-name drugs
* Preferred Specialty (Tier 3): includes specialty drugs for chronic, complex, or rare conditions
* Non-Preferred (Tier 4): includes generic, brand, and specialty drugs that are lower value than those in Tier 1, 2, or 3.

Once you have satisfied your medical deductible, you will pay your applicable coinsurance until you reach your out-of-pocket maximum for the plan year.
